name: briefing_runtime_destructor
description: HudUiBriefingRuntime::Destructor disables the runtime, resets locators, destroys message entries and briefing panels, tears down transport progress and action queue nodes, and destroys the background base.
known_limits: Binary Ninja reconstruction remains limited by explicit MSVC destructor EH registration/state scaffolding and the decompiler's circular-list boolean idiom; assembly and MLIL validate the cleanup order.

The native smoke covers non-empty message entries and action queue teardown with zeroed panel/background resource subobjects; non-null background image/video resource branches are covered by accepted direct callees.
functional_evidence: Binary Ninja assembly/decompilation for 0x403ed0 validates installing g_HudUiBriefingRuntime_FTable, calling HudUiBackground::SetEnabled(0), array-destroying six locator panels via HudUiElement::ResetCommonFTable, scalar-destroying message entries with flags 0, freeing and clearing the message vector, destroying panels in reverse construction order, destroying transport progress, unlinking and deleting all action queue nodes from the sentinel tail, deleting the sentinel, clearing action queue fields, and finally calling HudUiBackground::Destructor.

The native smoke initializes six locator panels, two message entries with a test scalar destructor, and a two-node circular action queue, then validates locator ftable reset, entry destructor calls and vector clearing, action queue clearing, transport-progress demotion, and background-base demotion.
